meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve no coding experience (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no coding experience. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debug Code Like a Pro: AI-Powered Workflow for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Master AI-driven debugging to overcome lack of coding skills. A high-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your freelance delivery.
content.answer_block: Stop letting syntax errors stall your freelance projects. By integrating Antigravity for architectural visualization and Claude Code for iterative error resolution, you can bypass traditional coding barriers. This workflow transforms complex debugging into a structured, automated process, allowing you to ship production-ready applications with zero manual code writing experience.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, the client deadline is looming, and your codebase is throwing a cascade of cryptic errors you don't understand. For the non-technical freelancer, this is the 'valley of death'—the moment where momentum dies and credibility is lost. You aren't paid to be a compiler; you are paid to deliver results.

But the rules of the game have changed. By leveraging the synthesis of Antigravity's structural oversight and Claude Code's deep reasoning capabilities, you can turn your lack of coding experience into a competitive advantage. It is time to stop fearing the stack trace and start orchestrating AI to build the architecture you envision.

steps.title: Establish the Architectural Blueprint
steps.description: Before debugging, visualize the logic flow. Use Antigravity to map out your application's intent, ensuring the AI understands the desired state versus the current broken reality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project directory. Map out the primary business logic flow and identify the specific module causing the current runtime exception.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Analyze the logic flow and identify the root cause of the current runtime error.'

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Points
steps.description: Don't ask the AI to fix 'everything.' Narrow the scope to specific functions or API endpoints to prevent hallucinated patches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a summary of the failing function's inputs and expected outputs versus actual outputs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Isolate the specific lines causing the error and provide a minimal reproduction test case.'

steps.title: Execute Iterative Refinement
steps.description: Use Claude Code to apply surgical fixes. By working in small loops, you maintain control over the codebase integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare the proposed fix against the initial architectural requirements to ensure no regression.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --apply --task 'Implement the fix for the isolated error and verify with the existing test suite.'

steps.title: Automated Validation Testing
steps.description: Force the AI to verify its own work. Never assume a fix works until the unit tests pass.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the newly patched module to confirm stability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Run all unit tests and report any remaining failures or edge cases.'

steps.title: Final Integration and Deployment
steps.description: Merge the fix back into the main branch and prepare for production, ensuring the documentation stays synced with the logic changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a brief technical summary of the fix for the client's documentation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Finalize code changes and document the fix in the README.md file.'

pro_tips: Always force the AI to explain the 'why' behind a fix; if it can't explain it, the fix is likely a hallucinated patch.
Use Antigravity to keep a 'System Context' file that tracks your project's high-level goals to prevent AI drift.
Never accept the first patch; ask for three different approaches and select the one with the lowest technical debt.
Treat your error logs as the primary prompt input; feed the raw stack trace directly into the Claude CLI for the most accurate diagnosis.
Implement a 'rollback-first' policy where you commit your state before allowing the AI to run any automated modification commands.
content.next_steps: Once your first bug is squashed, begin building a library of your own 'Golden Prompts' that you can reuse across different freelance projects to increase your delivery speed.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the toolkit to bypass technical bottlenecks and deliver high-end software regardless of your coding background. The gap between idea and execution is now smaller than ever. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
